description_long: This is a traditional axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a large, wedge-shaped metal head. The handle appears to be made from a light-colored hardwood, showing some wear and a natural grain pattern. The axe head is forged from dark, weathered steel, with a broad cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side. The blade is thick and heavy, designed for splitting or chopping, and is securely mounted onto the handle with a visible wooden wedge at the top. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian tool, likely used for heavy-duty tasks such as chopping wood or clearing debris. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, emphasizing its practical, workmanlike design.
description: A sturdy, traditional axe with a light wooden handle and a heavy, dark steel head, designed for chopping or splitting tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped steel blade.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the top of the axe head, the wooden wedge securing the blade, and the thickness of the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the flat poll and the wide cutting edge of the blade.
description_view_3: Rear angled view focusing on the end of the handle and the back of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved, cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth but worn surface and oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Wedge-shaped, single-edged steel blade with a broad cutting edge and a flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a visible wooden wedge securing the blade to the handle.
